summary: Kimi Antonelli seizes the initiative at Spa with pole position, immediately signaling Mercedes' intent to challenge Verstappen's dominance and setting up a straight fight between the Silver Arrows' pace and the championship leader's racecraft. Lewis Hamilton's Spanish momentum arrives at Ferrari's doorstep just as the Scuderia desperately needs to build on their recent resurgence, while Lando Norris faces a 10-place grid penalty that could reshape the midfield battle. A victory here would serve as a watershed moment—for Antonelli to announce himself as a serious championship contender, for Ferrari to prove their Barcelona breakthrough wasn't a one-off, or for Verstappen to reassert his grip on the title fight from adversity.
